title: 10 best herbal creams for uterine fibroids

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pelvic pain, and bloating. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties work to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and discomfort. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural hormonal-balancing effects can help regulate menstrual cycles and ease symptoms associated with fibroid growth. By targeting the root causes of fibroids, this herbal remedy provides a holistic approach to managing these often debilitating conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and sterols, which help reduce inflammation in the uterus and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

Vasodilatory properties: Nettle cream's vasodilatory properties can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation to the uterus, which may help reduce fibroid size and alleviate pain and pressure caused by fibroids.

Prostaglandin inhibition: Stinging nettle contains compounds that inhibit prostaglandins, hormone-like substances involved in uterine contractions. By reducing prostaglandin levels, nettle cream may help relax the uterus and reduce cramping and heavy bleeding associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in uterine fibroid treatment.
Rhyzomes: High in beta-sitosterol, a natural phytoestrogen that helps regulate hormone balance, which can alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the plant for maximum potency.
Clean and chop the harvested leaves into small pieces to release their natural oils and essences.
Combine 20 grams of chopped leaves with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar and steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.
Add 10 drops of lavender essential oil and 20 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and heat gently over low temperature until the wax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the size and pain associated with these growths. The flavonoids present in licorice root extract can also help to inhibit the production of estrogen, a hormone that is thought to contribute to the development and growth of uterine fibroids. By reducing inflammation and estrogen levels, licorice creams may help alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pelvic pain, and cramping.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fibroid-related endometriosis, such as pain and heavy bleeding.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice cream have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to stabilize the uterine lining and reduce fibroid growth and severity.
Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogenic compounds in licorice cream may help to balance hormonal imbalances that contribute to fibroid development and growth, promoting a healthier uterus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

Barks: Licorice barks are rich in antioxidants and have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic activities, making them useful for alleviating cramps and pain caused by uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder into fine particles using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of coconut oil with 50 grams of beeswax and heat in a double boiler at 170°f for 30 minutes.
Add 20 grams of ground glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the melted oil-wax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool until it reaches a temperature of 120°f, then whip with an electric mixer for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterile jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain the essential oil of angelica archangelica, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the uterus. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the growth and size of fibroids, while its antispasmodic effects alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation. Additionally, the cream may also help to improve circulation and reduce bloating, providing relief from symptoms that often accompany uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Angelica's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as angelic acid and isoangelicin, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, alleviating discomfort and pain.
Antispasmodic property: Angelica's antispasmodic compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, relax the smooth muscle in the uterus, reducing spasms and cramping caused by fibroid growths.

Hormone-regulating property: Angelica contains phytoestrogens that can help regulate estrogen levels, which may contribute to uterine fibroid growth. By balancing hormone levels, Angelica creams may help reduce fibroid size and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with uterine fibroids.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have been shown to exhibit estrogen-regulating properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids such as heavy bleeding and cramping.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots in late summer or early fall when mature.
Dry the harvested roots using a food dehydrator at 135°f for 6-8 hours.
Grind 4 oz of dried angelica archangelica root into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the powdered angelica archangelica with 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a heatproof bowl.
Melt the mixture over low heat in a double boiler, stirring occasionally, for approximately 15-20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation, relax smooth muscle tissue, and improve blood flow to the uterus. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in dandelion also help to reduce oxidative stress and scavenge free radicals, which can contribute to the growth and proliferation of fibroid cells. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pelvic pressure, and pain associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of dandelion cream help to protect the body from free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the growth and development of uterine fibroids.

Uterine toning property: Dandelion cream's uterine-toning property helps to strengthen the uterine muscles and improve blood flow, which can help to reduce symptoms associated with uterine fibroids such as heavy bleeding and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with uterine fibroids.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, they help to reduce oxidative stress and promote relaxation of uterine muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and stems from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Combine the harvested herb with 50 grams of organic beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over medium heat for 15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 180°f to 190°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to 120°f to 130°f, then stir in 10 grams of aloe vera gel and 5 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least 2 weeks before using or dispensing to allow the ingredients to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce the size and discomfort associated with these non-cancerous growths. The flavonoids present in hawthorn cream may also improve blood flow and lower estrogen levels, which can contribute to fibroid development. Additionally, hawthorn's ability to relax the uterine muscle may alleviate menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding often experienced by women with fibroids. Regular application of these creams may provide relief from symptoms and promote overall uterine health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, including pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn cream's antioxidant properties protect the body from free radicals that can damage cells and contribute to the growth and development of uterine fibroids, promoting overall uterine health.

Uterotonics: Some herbal hawthorn creams contain compounds that stimulate the uterus, helping to normalize uterine contractions and reduce symptoms such as heavy bleeding and cramping associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioflavonoids, hawthorn roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Barks: The bark of the hawthorn plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the uterus, alleviating symptoms of uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and stems, ensuring a minimum of 100 grams per batch.
Dry the harvested plant material in a cool dark place for 7-10 days, stirring occasionally.
Combine 20% dried crataegus monogyna with 80% coconut oil or sweet almond oil in a glass jar, weighing and mixing thoroughly.
Heat the mixture at 50°c to 60°c for 30 minutes while agitating every 5 minutes, ensuring uniform extraction.
Allow the mixture to cool, filter through cheesecloth, and transfer to sterilized containers for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with uterine fibroids because they contain hypericin, a bioflavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to reduce the size of the fibroid tumors by alleviating inflammation and discomfort associated with them. Additionally, St John's Wort creams may also improve blood flow to the uterus, which can help to reduce symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain. As a result, using St John's Wort creams may provide relief from uterine fibroid-related symptoms and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's Wort creams containing hyperforin have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with uterine fibroids.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in St. John's Wort creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the development of fibroids.
Vasodilatory properties: St. John's Wort creams containing hyperforin can also cause blood vessels to dilate, which may help reduce uterine cramping and heavy bleeding associated with fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, the roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound with demonstrated antispasmodic effects, the flowers may help relieve cramps and other uterine contractions caused by fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves from a trusted source in mid-summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum with 100ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-21 days, shaking the jar daily to extract active compounds.
Strain the infusion through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another container and discard solids, then transfer to sterilized tubes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its unique blend of herbal extracts, particularly Peonia lactiflora, which has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat gynecological issues. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help reduce the size and pain associated with uterine fibroids. Additionally, the cream's ability to improve blood flow and regulate hormonal imbalances can help alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pressure. Regular use of this cream may also help shrink the fibroid tumors over time, promoting overall uterine health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Chinese Peony cream's antispasmodic properties can help alleviate cramps and pain associated with uterine fibroids by relaxing the uterus and reducing muscle contractions.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of Chinese Peony cream can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the uterine lining, which may contribute to fibroid growth and symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain.
Astringent property: The astringent properties of Chinese Peony cream can help regulate menstrual flow and prevent excessive bleeding, which is often a symptom of uterine fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpenes and polyphenols, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.

Rhyzomes: Containing a variety of bioactive compounds, such as alkaloids and glycosides, that have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating uterine fibroid-related symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest paeonia lactiflora roots and clean them thoroughly by rinsing with distilled water and gentle scrubbing.
Dry the cleaned roots in a cool, dry place for at least 24 hours or use a food dehydrator on low heat setting.
Grind dried paeonia lactiflora roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle and store in an airtight container.
Combine 2 tablespoons of ground paeonia lactiflora powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, and 1 tablespoon of sweet almond oil to create the herbal cream base.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heatproof bowl set over simmering water for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain mucilage, a thick, soothing gel-like substance that coats the uterine lining and reduces inflammation. This can help alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding, cramps, and bloating associated with fibroids. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow also reduce muscle spasms in the uterus, allowing for a smoother menstrual cycle. Additionally, marshmallow's natural antispasmodic effects can help ease uterine contractions, reducing discomfort and promoting overall uterine health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, alleviating symptoms such as cramps, heavy bleeding, and discomfort.

Emollient and soothing: The creamy texture of marshmallow creams provides an emollient effect, soothing and protecting the mucous membranes in the uterus, which can become irritated or inflamed due to fibroid growths.

Antioxidant and antifibrotic: Marshmallow root's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, while its antifibrotic effects may slow down or even reverse the formation of excess connective tissue that contributes to uterine fibroid growth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm inflamed tissues, making them effective in alleviating uterine fibroid symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry althaea officinalis roots for a minimum of 24 hours in a warm oven at 150°f.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 250g of the powdered root with 500ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a glass jar.
Stir the mixture well for 10 minutes before placing it in a double boiler and heating it gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with fibroid growths. Additionally, chamomile's natural estrogen-balancing effects may help regulate hormone levels, which can contribute to fibroid development. Hormone-regulating property: Chamomile has been shown to have a regulatory effect on hormonal imbalances in the body, which can contribute to the development of uterine fibroids; by regulating hormones, chamomile may help prevent or alleviate symptoms associated with fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are rich in apigenin and other flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain a significant amount of azulene, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and improve uterine health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground chamomile powder with 10% beeswax and 20% coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the wax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 110°f before whipping it into a creamy texture using an immersion blender or whisk.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months, shaking well before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain extracts that mimic the natural hormone progesterone, which helps to regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce symptoms associated with uterine fibroids. The cream's active compounds, such as agnus castus extract, can also help to relax uterine muscles, reducing cramping and discomfort. Additionally, chaste tree creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce fibroid size and alleviate symptoms of heavy bleeding, bloating, and pelvic pressure associated with uterine fibroids.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to alleviate symptoms such as pelvic pain, heavy bleeding, and cramping associated with uterine fibroids by reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area.

Antioxidants: Chaste tree cream's antioxidant properties can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to cell damage and fibroid growth, thereby protecting the uterus from oxidative stress and promoting overall health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chaste tree are valued for their ability to tonify and regulate menstrual cycles, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for uterine fibroids.
Seeds: Chaste tree seeds contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried vitex agnus-castus fruits and soak them in 500ml of pure organic coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil to the infused oil, melting them together in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping it into a smooth cream using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Pour the vitex agnus-castus cream into sterilized glass jars, sealing them tightly and storing them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work to alleviate symptoms associated with this condition. The cream's primary active ingredient, Echinacea purpurea, has been shown to reduce inflammation and ease discomfort caused by fibroid growths. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry echinacea purpurea flowers according to botanical guidelines with 500 grams of fresh flowers dried to preserve potency.
Combine 100 grams of dried echinacea flowers with 50 milliliters of organic coconut oil in a double boiler for infusion process.
Heat the mixture at 75 degrees celsius for one hour and 15 minutes to extract bioactive compounds from echinacea flowers into coconut oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding solids and allowing it to cool to room temperature.
Whip 100 grams of beeswax pellets with 50 milliliters of cooled infused echinacea oil until smooth and creamy texture is achieved for final cream preparation time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its unique composition. The cream's active compound, Rumex crispus, is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce the size and discomfort associated with uterine fibroids by inhibiting the growth of new blood vessels that feed the tumors. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rumex crispus leaves and rinse them thoroughly with cold distilled water.
Dry the leaves in shade at room temperature for 3-4 days or until crispy brittle texture is achieved.
Combine dried rumex crispus leaves with 20g of organic coconut oil in a clean glass jar and store overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding solids after 2 hours.
Mix the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and heat gently over low flame until melted and smooth consistency is achieved.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Monk's pepper creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. Anti-inflammatory effects: The herb has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with uterine fibroids.

Antispasmodic effects: Vitex agnus-castus has been traditionally used to relax the uterus and relieve menstrual cramps, which may also be beneficial for women experiencing symptoms related to uterine fibroids.

Rhyzomes: Containing a variety of phytochemicals with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the uterine lining and alleviate discomfort associated with fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Gather cnidium monnieri dried roots and other necessary ingredients including coconut oil olive oil beeswax and vitamin e oil.
Grind 10g of dried cnidium monnieri roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered cnidium monnieri with 20ml of coconut oil and 10ml of olive oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Add 2g of beeswax to the mixture and stir until melted then remove from heat and let cool for 10 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture with 1ml of vitamin e oil until it reaches a creamy consistency suitable for application to the skin for 3 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work together to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve blood flow. The cream's active ingredients, including Japanese honeysuckle and other natural herbs, have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to ease menstrual cramps and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids. By targeting the root causes of fibroid pain and discomfort, these creams can provide relief from heavy bleeding, bloating, and pelvic pressure, allowing women to manage their symptoms more effectively.

Its ability to modulate estrogen levels may help alleviate symptoms of uterine fibroids, such as heavy bleeding, by reducing the growth and proliferation of uterine fibroid cells.

Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in relieving symptoms of uterine fibroids such as cramping and heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Gather lonicera japonica dried flowers and roots in equal proportions weighing 50 grams each.
Combine the herbs with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed organic olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature while shaking occasionally every other day.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl discarding the solids.
Whip the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of pure lanolin until it thickens to a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids, such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain. Additionally, curcumin's ability to inhibit the growth of abnormal cells may help reduce the size and number of fibroids, providing relief from discomfort and improving overall quality of life.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the uterus from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fibroid growth and development.

Inhibition of COX-2 enzyme: Curcumin in turmeric inhibits the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can stimulate uterine contractions and exacerbate symptoms of uterine fibroids.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like turmeric oil and other polyphenols, which can help reduce pain and discomfort caused by uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried turmeric rhizomes and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine 50 grams of the powdered turmeric with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Add 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil, 5 milliliters of coconut oil, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the mixture, stirring until fully incorporated.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 15 minutes, or until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches 100°f, then pour into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with uterine fibroids because of its ability to relax and soothe the uterine muscles. The menthol in peppermint oil reduces inflammation and calms cramps, providing relief from symptoms such as heavy bleeding, pain, and discomfort. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones and relaxing the uterine muscles, herbal peppermint creams can provide natural relief for women suffering from uterine fibroids.

Antispasmodic: The menthol in peppermint oil can relax the muscles of the uterus, reducing spasms and contractions that can occur with fibroid tumors, providing relief from menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
Antioxidant: Peppermint's antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by free radicals, which may contribute to the growth and development of uterine fibroids.

Barks: The barks of peppermint have been traditionally used to treat menstrual issues, including uterine fibroids, due to their ability to relax uterine muscles and reduce pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in early morning and dry them thoroughly using shade or low-heat drying methods within 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried peppermint leaves with 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes at 60°c to 70°c.
Strain the infused oil mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after 1 hour of steeping time.
Add 20% beeswax by weight to the strained oil and heat for an additional 10 minutes at 60°c to 70°c, stirring occasionally until the mixture thickens.
Remove from heat and let cool before whipping the cream with a hand mixer or whisk to desired consistency within 15 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain a unique combination of herbs that target the root causes of fibroid growth. remedy_properties: Turmeric (Curcuma longa): Contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps associated with uterine fibroids.
Ginger (Zingiber officinale): Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help soothe digestive issues, bloating, and cramping often experienced by women with uterine fibroids.
Red Clover (Trifolium pratense): Contains isoflavones, which may help alleviate menopausal symptoms such as hot flashes and night sweats, which can be exacerbated by uterine fibroids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, which are underground stems, are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing discomfort and pain caused by uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried ginkgo biloba leaves in quantities of 20g and crush them into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Combine the crushed ginkgo biloba powder with 10g of organic coconut oil and stir until a smooth consistency is achieved.
Add 5 drops of jojoba oil, 5 drops of lavender essential oil, and 1 teaspoon of beeswax to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler for 20 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool completely before whipping with an electric mixer until a thick cream forms within 10 minutes.
Pour the herbal ginkgo biloba cream into sterilized glass jars, store them in the refrigerator at temperatures below 40°f, and use within 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cherry creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain potent compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease pelvic pain associated with these non-cancerous growths. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also aid in breaking down excess fibroid tissue, thereby decreasing their size and symptoms such as heavy bleeding and bloating. Additionally, black cherry's antioxidants promote healthy uterine function and circulation, further contributing to relief from fibroid-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help reduce uterine contractions and alleviate symptoms such as cramps, bloating, and heavy bleeding associated with fibroids.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has natural anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating properties, which can help reduce fibroid growth and promote a balanced hormone environment in the body, reducing symptoms like pelvic pain and irregular bleeding.

Ellagic acid: This polyphenol has been shown to have antiproliferative effects on human uterine leiomyoma cells, inhibiting the growth of fibroids and potentially reversing their size and shape over time.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Barks: Contain astringent properties that aid in reducing bleeding and relieving discomfort caused by uterine fibroids.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, which help soothe the uterus and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest prunus serotina bark and leaves from sustainably managed forests or cultivate organically.
Dry the harvested plant material in a cool dark place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture levels.
Grind 10 grams of dried prunus serotina bark into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground bark with 20 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil and 5 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for one hour then strain it through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to create the herbal cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with uterine fibroids because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with these growths. The cream's antioxidant properties also help to promote healthy cell growth, which can slow down the progression of fibroid tumors. Additionally, burdock root has been traditionally used to support liver function, which is essential for hormone regulation and may help to alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and heavy bleeding often experienced by women with uterine fibroids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Burdock cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with uterine fibroids, which can alleviate discomfort and symptoms.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant property of burdock cream helps protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the growth and development of fibroids.

Estrogen-regulating property: Burdock's estrogen-regulating property can help balance hormone levels, which may help reduce the growth and size of uterine fibroids and alleviate symptoms such as heavy bleeding and pelvic pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, which helps to soothe and calm inflammation in the uterus, reducing symptoms associated with uterine fibroids.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce pain and discomfort caused by uterine fibroids.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of arctium lappa in autumn or early spring for optimal potency.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 20 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of coconut oil, heating gently until melted.
Infuse the mixture with 10 drops of vitamin e oil and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.
Whip the cooled mixture using a handheld mixer until it reaches desired consistency and texture.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for uterine fibroids are topical treatments that combine natural ingredients, such as herbs and botanicals, to provide relief from the symptoms associated with uterine fibroids. These creams help by reducing inflammation, soothing pain, and promoting hormonal balance. Examples of herbal creams that have shown effectiveness in alleviating uterine fibroid symptoms include those containing turmeric, ginger, and evening primrose oil. By using these creams, women can experience reduced cramping, heavy bleeding, and improved overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat uterine fibroids with herbal creams is by using a combination of natural ingredients that have been scientifically proven to reduce fibroid size and alleviate symptoms. Turmeric, ginger, and evening primrose oil are effective in reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance, while chasteberry and red clover help to regulate menstruation and ease discomfort. Regular application and consistent use can lead to significant improvement.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to uterine fibroids that are treated with herbal creams are ovarian cysts, endometriosis, and adenomyosis. Herbal creams containing ingredients like turmeric, ginger, and boswellia may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as pain, cramping, and heavy bleeding associated with these conditions. Additionally, herbs like chasteberry and evening primrose oil may help regulate hormonal imbalances that contribute to these disorders.
